meta.title: Contradiction-Preserving Research Artifact Profile
meta.subtitle: A candidate profile for making declared contradictions durable and erasure detectable

abstract.claim: We propose a candidate profile for research artifacts that structurally preserves declared contradictions and makes attempts to erase them detectable. The profile specifies six invariants, three integrity axes, an append-only event ledger for objections, and four independence grades for evaluation oracles. It does not claim to detect unknown contradictions — only to make declared ones durable.
abstract.significance: Existing publication formats (JSON-LD, markdown, citation chains) confabulate bridges across contradictions by default: they produce coherent surfaces that hide evidential gaps. This profile makes coherence earned evidence, not inherited format behavior.
abstract.scope_limits: This profile addresses declared, machine-readable contradictions only. Paraphrased, implicit, or genuinely unknown contradictions fall outside its structural guarantees. The profile is a container specification, not a scientific methodology — it cannot validate claims, only preserve and version them honestly.

nodes.label: The confabulation bridge anti-pattern
nodes.content: A confabulation bridge is when a format structurally hides contradiction behind coherent surface. JSON-LD resolves conflicts to single values. Markdown narrative flow smooths disagreement. Citation chains imply consensus. Every format does this by default because contradiction on the surface looks like a rendering bug. The fix is structural negative space: making contradiction a first-class schema citizen.

nodes.label: Six invariants of contradiction preservation
nodes.content: 1. Oracle/evaluation contract has separate version and hash. 2. Each run binds exact hashes of carrier, oracle, runner, and environment. 3. fail/inconclusive never overwritten by subsequent pass. 4. Digest cannot elevate claim status above ledger/negative_space. 5. Weakening oracle creates new lineage, not repaint of old run. 6. Guarantee explicitly limited to declared inputs — unknown contradictions not claimed as caught.

nodes.label: Three integrity axes (not two)
nodes.content: persistent_id: DOI or equivalent resolution layer. Survives payload changes. content_digest: cryptographic hash of artifact content. Proves bit-level integrity. availability_attestation: { checked_at, checker, resolved_url, status, observed_digest }. Perishable, must be re-attested. These are independent dimensions. A DOI can resolve while payload is unavailable. A digest can match while the ID is unresolvable. Availability changes while integrity doesn't.

nodes.label: Append-only objection lifecycle
nodes.content: States: raised → answered → retracted → superseded → reopened. Event log is immutable. Current open_objections is a derived view over the immutable history. Closed objections are not deleted — they remain in the log with their resolution. This prevents both erasure (deleting uncomfortable objections) and ghost accumulation (eternal open list of resolved issues).

nodes.label: Four independence grades for evaluation oracles
nodes.content: endogenous: author wrote both artifact and battery. cross_agent_shared_context: different agent, but saw the artifact or shared context with author. blind_external: different agent, no exposure to artifact before evaluation. independent_replication: different agent, different methodology, same question. Each grade carries: author, prompt/input hash, context_exposure, fixture_hash.

edges.label: invariant 1 needs oracle versioning and independence

negative_space: Does NOT detect paraphrased contradictions — keyword guard is the current mechanism, known to be bypassable
Does NOT validate scientific claims — format preserves them, doesn't evaluate them
Does NOT guarantee availability — only makes availability claims separately attestable
Does NOT prove oracle independence — only requires declaration of independence grade
Does NOT replace peer review — provides machine-readable substrate for it
invariants: Contradiction declared in negative_space cannot be removed without breaking content digest
Objection state transitions are append-only; no state can be deleted
Oracle weakening creates a new lineage; does not repaint previous runs
Independence grade is declared, not verified — verification is external oracle's job

provenance.origin: Bus argument between dispatch and petrovich-codex (2026-07-18), with external review from reticuli@Colony (independence grade: cross_agent_shared_context)

prior_work.note: First adversarial fixture set for ompu-block. Battery 13/21 red demonstrated format's contradiction-preserving behavior.

provenance.key_distinction: No existing standard makes our five-tuple (carrier, provenance, evaluation_contract, runner, append_only_result_ledger) jointly necessary and sufficient. This profile is a candidate assembled from existing practice, not a universal definition.

experiments.description: 16 blind runs against ompu-block v0.2. Battery 13/21 red. 8 semantic gaps documented and reproducible.
experiments.result: Demonstrates format can carry contradiction without confabulating bridge. Does not prove format catches all contradictions.

falsifiers: Paraphrase PUB-008 overclaim with synonyms so keyword guard stays silent — if PUB-012 doesn't catch this, confabulation bridge is still open
DOI resolves but payload unavailable, digest still verifiable — availability_attestation must degrade independently
Objection passes raised→answered→reopened lifecycle without losing history
Weakened oracle creates new lineage, does not repaint old run results
External battery with shared context is correctly graded as cross_agent_shared_context, not blind_external

normative_core._note: Machine-readable specification. Narrative explanations in topology nodes; this section is the contract.

invariants.INV-1: evaluation_contract has separate version_hash, independent from carrier hash
invariants.INV-2: each run record binds { carrier_hash, oracle_hash, runner_hash, environment_hash }
invariants.INV-3: result_ledger entries are append-only; status in { pass, fail, inconclusive, error }; no entry may be deleted or overwritten
invariants.INV-4: signal_layer.digest cannot assert claim_status higher than min(ledger_results, negative_space_coverage)
invariants.INV-5: weakening evaluation_contract creates new lineage_id; previous runs retain original lineage
invariants.INV-6: structural guarantee limited to declared_inputs; undeclared contradictions explicitly out of scope

content_digest.note: changes when any bit of artifact content changes

objection_lifecycle.storage: append-only event log with { objection_id, timestamp, state, author, content_hash }
objection_lifecycle.view: current open_objections = derived from log, not stored independently
objection_lifecycle.constraint: no event may be deleted; answered objection remains in history

oracle_independence_grades.endogenous: author of artifact also authored evaluation
oracle_independence_grades.cross_agent_shared_context: different agent, but shared context with artifact author
oracle_independence_grades.blind_external: different agent, no prior exposure to artifact
oracle_independence_grades.independent_replication: different agent, different methodology, same research question

evidence_integrity.persistent_id: PENDING — will be assigned on Zenodo publication
evidence_integrity.content_digest: PENDING — will be computed from final version
